actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour (script_fileID: 1342851255, GUID: df6ed34bdc198f845aeef08129f7eb31) is DoorController with serialization config: openAngle=90, animationSpeed=3, startLocked=1. Initial isLocked=true, so Toggle should have no effect. No m_Calls are specified in scene meta; manual trigger required.

Unlock must be called before Open/Toggle have any effect. MonoBehaviour (script_fileID: 1342851255, GUID: df6ed34bdc198f845aeef08129f7eb31) has startLocked=1, so we first Unlock, then Open. Expect [DoorController] DoorPivot unlocked log, then door begins lerping toward +90° Y open rotation.
Door is now unlocked (from previous step). Close should always set isOpen=false regardless of lock state, causing door to lerp back to closedRotation.
After Unlock, Toggle should flip isOpen state each call. With isLocked=false, Toggle should alternate between opening and closing the door via Update lerp.

MonoBehaviour (id=746320263) is KeyUnlockReceiver (script guid=c87167e8859f9dc4d98e818aab1910b1) attached to GameObject 'KeySocket' (fileID=746320259). It registers in Awake to XRSocketInteractor.selectEntered on component fileID=746320261. Serialized refs: doorController=fileID 1342851255, socketIndicatorRenderer=fileID 864473226, unlockedMaterial guid=7c38ca2c8b284d9439e0aeac6916567c. Since 'KeySocket' has a SphereCollider (isTrigger=1) and no Rigidbody, we cannot use Grab/Transform to naturally insert an interactable. Manually trigger the expected outcome of selectEntered by calling DoorController.Unlock(), which KeyUnlockReceiver would invoke upon item insertion. Expected behavior: hasUnlocked becomes true, door unlocked, socketIndicatorRenderer.sharedMaterial set to unlockedMaterial, and Debug.Log prints "[KeyUnlockReceiver] Item inserted — door unlocked!".

Manual call to simulate XRSimpleInteractable.selectEntered because no m_Calls wiring is provided. LightSwitch_Room1 has BoxCollider (id: 1894443797) allowing Trigger. MonoBehaviour_0 (id: 1894443799, guid: afb52d2e54ea1614294036b01b20569f) is LightSwitchController with serialized fields: controlledLight fileID 590520313, switchRenderer fileID 1894443796, litMaterial guid 1529549f84e419e409d02128368bed06, unlitMaterial guid 25cacebe91df7a9478efb7354f75efdf. Expect Toggle to flip controlledLight.enabled and update material.
Second manual Toggle to return to original state and cover both visual/material branches (lit and unlit) and light enabled/disabled states for controlledLight (fileID 590520313).

Room2 (fileID 1271916000) currently shows no Rigidbody, Collider, EventTrigger, or attached MonoBehaviour with UnityEvent (m_Calls) in the provided scene JSON. We will only add manual Trigger actions for UnityEvent m_Calls defined on MonoBehaviour components if they exist. Please provide the MonoBehaviour components (including script fileID, GUID, and m_Calls) attached to Room2 and its children so we can target public no-arg methods. We will treat the provided MonoBehaviour component data as the exact settings of the attached scripts to be tested.

MonoBehaviour (id 659474796) on Drawer (fileID 659474793) is the exact scene settings of the attached script DrawerController (m_Script guid 181a93ff91c685946b20f54ea877474b) with openDistance=0.42 and animationSpeed=5. Drawer has a BoxCollider (id 659474798) and no Rigidbody, so Trigger action is valid. XR selectEntered would normally call Toggle(), but to ensure coverage we manually invoke the public no-arg method Toggle() twice: first to OPEN, then after 1.0s to CLOSE, exercising both states and the Lerp motion handled in Update. Drawer’s Transform is parented under Cabinet (m_Father fileID 134420580) within Room2.

MonoBehaviour (id 1618227218) on LightSwitch_Room2 (fileID 1618227212) is the exact scene settings of the attached script LightSwitchController (m_Script guid afb52d2e54ea1614294036b01b20569f, type 3) with serialized refs: controlledLight=fileID 340544673, switchRenderer=fileID 1618227215, litMaterial guid 1529549f84e419e409d02128368bed06, unlitMaterial guid 25cacebe91df7a9478efb7354f75efdf. LightSwitch_Room2 has a BoxCollider (id 1618227216) and no Rigidbody, so Trigger is valid. XR selectEntered would normally invoke Toggle(), but to ensure coverage we manually call the public no-arg method Toggle() twice to exercise both ON and OFF states and the material swap.
